_comment: preToolUse hook for the hardening lab. Emits ONLY `deny` when toolName is outside the allow-list. `allow` and `ask` decisions are DELIBERATELY OMITTED: per https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/hooks-configuration the runtime currently honors only `deny`; `allow`/`ask` outputs are accepted but ignored, so designing around them gives false confidence. Absence of deny is allow. Note on toolName aliases: the same shell-execution tool may surface as `shell`, `execute`, or `bash` depending on which plugin/extension registered it — because this is an allow-list (default-deny), all three aliases are blocked automatically. If you ever invert the polarity to a deny-list, you MUST enumerate all three aliases or one will bypass the gate.

preToolUse.bash: INPUT=$(cat); TOOL=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.toolName // empty' 2>/dev/null); case "$TOOL" in ""|read|microsoft-learn/microsoft_docs_search) exit 0 ;; esac; REASON="tool '$TOOL' is not in the hardening allow-list (read, microsoft-learn/microsoft_docs_search). Widen the allow-list in .github/hooks/deny-unlisted-tools.json only after a security review."; jq -nc --arg r "$REASON" '{permissionDecision:"deny", permissionDecisionReason:$r}'; exit 0
preToolUse.powershell: $raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd(); try { $evt = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { exit 0 }; $tool = [string]$evt.toolName; if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($tool)) { exit 0 }; $allow = @('read','microsoft-learn/microsoft_docs_search'); if ($allow -contains $tool) { exit 0 }; $reason = "tool '$tool' is not in the hardening allow-list (read, microsoft-learn/microsoft_docs_search). Widen the allow-list in .github/hooks/deny-unlisted-tools.json only after a security review."; @{ permissionDecision = 'deny'; permissionDecisionReason = $reason } | ConvertTo-Json -Compress; exit 0
preToolUse.comment: Deny-only guardrail. Defense-in-depth against a mis-edited agent frontmatter. Allow-list mirrors solutions/lab-hardening/tight-reviewer.agent.md.
